title: MCP-AQL Danger Level
description: Schema for operation danger classification in MCP-AQL adapters

x-enum-descriptions.safe: Read-only operations with no side effects. Always safe to execute automatically. Maps to READ endpoint operations (list, get, search, introspect).
x-enum-descriptions.low: Additive operations that are easily reversible. Creates new state without modifying existing state. Maps to CREATE endpoint operations (create, add, upload, register).
x-enum-descriptions.moderate: Modifying operations that are potentially reversible. Changes existing state but original values may be recoverable. Maps to UPDATE endpoint operations (update, edit, set, rename).
x-enum-descriptions.high: Destructive operations that are difficult or impossible to reverse. Removes state permanently. Maps to DELETE endpoint operations (delete, remove, purge, clear).
x-enum-descriptions.forbidden: Operations that should never be auto-executed under any circumstances. Reserved for critical system operations, bulk destructive actions, or operations with severe consequences. Requires explicit human confirmation regardless of context.

DangerClassification.title: Danger Classification
DangerClassification.description: Complete danger classification for an operation including level and metadata

level.description: The danger level classification for this operation

reason.description: Human-readable explanation for why this operation has this danger classification

requires_confirmation.description: UI hint indicating whether the operation should prompt for user confirmation before execution

hook_blockable.description: Whether pre-execution hooks can block this operation. When false, hooks receive notification but cannot prevent execution

EndpointDangerDefaults.title: Endpoint Danger Defaults
EndpointDangerDefaults.description: Default danger level mappings for CRUDE endpoints

READ.description: READ operations default to safe (read-only, no side effects)

CREATE.description: CREATE operations default to low (additive, easily reversible)

UPDATE.description: UPDATE operations default to moderate (modifying, potentially reversible)

DELETE.description: DELETE operations default to high (destructive, difficult to reverse)

EXECUTE.description: EXECUTE operations vary from safe to forbidden depending on the specific operation
